# /spec

> Draft a feature spec for a reporting deliverable (Power BI dataset / dataflow / report / dashboard / app, or CE SSRS report). Spec is feature-scoped; FDD/TDD/blueprint are domain-scoped.

## Usage

```
/spec --feature <slug> [--source fresh|alm|handoff] [--project <name>]
```

## Inputs

- `agents.yaml`, `project.config.yaml reporting.*`, `project.config.yaml locales`
- [templates/spec.template.md](../../templates/spec.template.md)
- Source: business KPI doc / RFP / user story / handoff manifest

## Execution flow

1. Validate slug; scaffold via `New-Feature.ps1` if needed.
2. Read project config for workspace name, refresh windows, gateway, RLS policy.
3. Render `spec.template.md` with sections:
   - §1 Business context (what decisions this reporting supports)
   - §2 Personas (analyst / executive / operator / partner)
   - §3 FRs (e.g., FR-01 "Customer Aging report with drill-through to detail")
   - §4 NFR (refresh window, dataset size, RLS coverage, accessibility WCAG AA)
   - §5 Reporting layer (dataset / dataflow / report / dashboard / app — pattern selection)
   - §6 Data sourcing (Dataverse direct / BYOD / Synapse Link / data lake — per [constitution/03-data-sourcing.md](../../constitution/03-data-sourcing.md))
   - §7 Security (RLS / OLS / sensitivity label)
   - §8 Out of scope
5. `currentStates=[SPEC_DRAFT]`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/reporting/features/{f}/spec.md`
